occurrence_sentence: Two research groups, including a quantum startup founded by UChicago Pritzker Molecular Engineering alumnus and one at U.S. Department of Energy's Argonne National Laboratory, have used different host materials for erbium to advance quantum technology.

mentions.occurrence_sentence: Two research groups, including a quantum startup founded by UChicago Pritzker Molecular Engineering alumnus and one at U.S. Department of Energy's Argonne National Laboratory, have used different host materials for erbium to advance quantum technology.

mentions.role_in_text: Alumnus Manish Singh is associated with the University of Chicago
mentions.occurrence_sentence: Scientists have found that an atom of erbiumвҖ”a rare-earth metal sometimes used in lasers or to color glassвҖ”can be a very effective qubit. To make erbium qubits, erbium atoms are placed in вҖңhost materials,вҖқ where the erbium atoms replace some of the material's original atoms. Two research groupsвҖ”one at quantum startup memQ, founded by UChicago Pritzker Molecular Engineering alumnus Manish Singh, and one at the U.S. Department of Energy's Argonne National LaboratoryвҖ”have used different host materials for erbium to advance quantum technology, demonstrating the versatility of this kind of qubit and highlighting the importance of materials science to quantum computing and quantum communication.
